reviews.comment: Nice, great, super intelligent man... Can not teach he just writes on the board and says, "Simple right." idk I would take any other professor unless you already know the material.
He is a very good and patient teacher. He never gets tired of explaining things and he will try to make you understand as best as he can if you go and talk to him. :)

His tone puts you to sleep, he is difficult to understand, and he isn't the most fluent in math person I've seen. He's nice and approachable, but he isn't a great teacher. He single handedly made me want to stop being a math  major.
He truly is a great teacher so long as you pay attention and ask if you have issues. I have never had any trouble picking up what he was putting down.

Dr. Lee is WONDERFUL! He is SO helpful and is very willing to work with you if you don't understand. He has been a great teacher. In my College Algebra w/ Modeling class, he would drop the lowest test grade if you didn't miss more than 5 times. He's a nice, helpful, great professor!

He's nice, very helpful, great at teaching from elementary to complex. If you already know all the elementary stuff... well i hope u don't fall asleep during class. He's rather easy, but u do have to do homework. His quizzes and tests are ok, if you practiced beforehand.
good guy, great at turning in assignments and such, not so great at teaching the material.
good guy, great at turning in assignments and such, not so great at teaching the material.

Best math teacher i've ever had.. take him if you have trouble with elementary stuff while having to move on.. he'll go as back as needed to explain everything to.. and of course, you have to do hw.. it's calculus!

He's a nice guy, but the class was harder than it needed to be. Honestly, he is hard to understand, which leaves the student to teach themselves. Also, he collect homework and THEN discusses it/ goes over it. That prohibits students from being able to have clarity and those "ah-ha" moments that are necessary to learning.
